title: eschscholzia minutiflora subsp. covillei
intro: Eschscholzia minutiflora subsp. covillei, also known as the San Diego evening primrose, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the evening primrose family found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its roots and flowers. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function and antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of Eschscholzia minutiflora subsp. covillei can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions and wounds.
